quoteTime: about four o'clock in the afternoon
quoteLast: , and Edward, as he walked on, said to himself, "That man must be of a very wicked disposition, for I have offended him in nothing except in not submitting to be made his prisoner; and is that an offence to take a man's life for?  He is a dangerous man, and will be more dangerous after being again foiled by me as he has been to-day.  I doubt if he will go home; I am almost sure that he will turn and follow me when he thinks that he can without my seeing him; and if he does, he will find out where our cottage is--and who knows what mischief he may not do, and how he may alarm my little sisters?  I'll not go home till dark; and I'll now walk in another direction, that I may mislead him."
title: The Children of the New Forest
author: Marryat, Frederick, 1792-1848
